papers.title: VideoKR: Towards Knowledge- and Reasoning-Intensive Video Understanding
papers.abstract: Abstract VideoKR presents a large-scale video reasoning dataset and benchmark designed to enhance knowledge-intensive video understanding through expert-domain content and human-in-the-loop example generation.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We introduce VideoKR, the first large-scale training corpus specifically designed to strengthen knowledge- and reasoning-intensive video understanding. It comprises 315K video reasoning examples over 145K newly collected, CC-licensed, expert-domain videos. We develop a human-in-the-loop, skill-oriented example generation pipeline that targets progressively deeper video reasoning capabilities while ensuring the difficulty, diversity, and reliability of both the examples and their CoT rationales. We also curate VideoKR-Eval, a new expert-annotated benchmark where questions require genuine video understanding and knowledge-intensive reasoning rather than textual shortcuts. Our experiments show that, under a standard SFTrightarrowGRPO pipeline, models post-trained on VideoKR outperform prior post-training approaches on knowledge-intensive video reasoning while remaining competitive on general video reasoning, highlighting data design as a key driver of progress in video reasoning. We further conduct comprehensive ablations to isolate the contributions of VideoKR, providing actionable insights for future work.
affiliations: University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing, China
Yale University, New Haven, CT, USA

ru.title: Качественные данные как ключ к прогрессу в видеорассуждениях
ru.desc: В работе представлен VideoKR — первый крупномасштабный набор данных для обучения моделей глубокому пониманию видеоконтента с использованием знаний и рассуждений. Датасет содержит 315K примеров рассуждений на основе 145K видео от экспертов, созданных с применением методологии human-in-the-loop для обеспечения высокого качества. Авторы разработали pipeline генерации примеров, ориентированный на постепенное усложнение задач рассуждения, с гарантией разнообразия и надежности как самих примеров, так и цепочек мышления (CoT). Эксперименты показывают, что модели, дообученные на VideoKR используя SFT и GRPO, значительно превосходят существующие подходы в задачах интенсивного рассуждения о видеоконтенте.

en.title: Enhancing Video Understanding with Knowledge-Driven Reasoning
en.desc: VideoKR is a large-scale dataset aimed at improving video reasoning by focusing on knowledge-intensive understanding. It includes 315,000 examples derived from 145,000 expert-domain videos, created through a human-in-the-loop process to ensure high quality and diversity. The dataset is designed to challenge models with complex reasoning tasks that require genuine understanding of video content. Experiments show that models trained on VideoKR significantly outperform previous methods in knowledge-intensive video reasoning, emphasizing the importance of well-structured data in advancing this field.

zh.title: 增强视频理解的知识推理数据集
zh.desc: VideoKR是一个大规模的视频推理数据集，旨在通过专家领域内容和人机协作生成示例来增强知识密集型视频理解。该数据集包含315,000个视频推理示例，基于145,000个新收集的、具有CC许可的专家领域视频。我们开发了一种人机协作的示例生成管道，旨在逐步提升视频推理能力，同时确保示例及其推理的难度、多样性和可靠性。实验结果表明，在标准的SFTrightarrowGRPO管道下，基于VideoKR进行后训练的模型在知识密集型视频推理上优于之前的后训练方法，同时在一般视频推理上也保持竞争力，突显了数据设计在视频推理进展中的关键作用。

papers.title: ArcANE: Do Role-Playing Language Agents Stay in Character at the Right Time?
papers.abstract: Abstract Role-playing language agents require dynamic character development that evolves through narratives, necessitating benchmarks that evaluate psychological trajectory alignment rather than static factual recall, with ArcANE demonstrating superior performance when character arc information is conditioned into models.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Role-playing language agents (RPLAs) should play characters whose values and behavior evolve as the story progresses, not maintain a fixed persona. Existing benchmarks measure factual recall at a given chapter, not whether responses align with the character's psychological trajectory, especially in scenarios the source text never explores. We introduce ArcANE (Arc-Aware Narrative Evaluation), an automatically constructed benchmark spanning 17 novels and 80 principal characters. A Character Arc segments the narrative into phases along a psychological axis, and each probe poses the same scenario across phases, spanning both situations within the source text and situations beyond it. Across six models and six context modes, conditioning on the Character Arc tops every other context strategy on every model, and the gap is largest on scenarios outside the source text where retrieval has nothing to find. We further fine-tune open-weight models on the same data to obtain ArcANE-8B/32B, which widen the Arc advantage even more on scenarios outside the source text.
affiliations: Graduate School of Data Science, Seoul National University

ru.title: Эволюция характера вместо статичной личности в языковых агентах
ru.desc: Статья представляет ArcANE — бенчмарк для оценки языковых моделей в задаче ролевого взаимодействия, где агенты должны развивать характер персонажа в соответствии с его психологической траекторией на протяжении повествования. В отличие от существующих подходов, которые измеряют только точность фактического припоминания, предложенный метод оценивает способность моделей сохранять согласованность поведения персонажа на разных этапах истории. Бенчмарк построен на основе 17 романов и включает 80 главных персонажей с разбиением на фазы психологического развития. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что обусловливание информацией о характере персонажа значительно превосходит другие стратегии контекстуализации, особенно для сценариев за пределами исходного текста.

en.title: Evolving Characters: The Key to Dynamic Role-Playing Agents
en.desc: This paper discusses the need for role-playing language agents (RPLAs) to develop characters that evolve over time, reflecting changes in values and behaviors as narratives progress. It introduces ArcANE, a new benchmark designed to evaluate how well these agents align with the psychological trajectories of characters rather than just recalling facts. The benchmark includes 17 novels and 80 characters, assessing responses to scenarios that may not be explicitly covered in the source material. The results show that conditioning models on character arc information significantly improves performance, especially in novel situations, highlighting the importance of dynamic character development in RPLAs.

zh.title: 动态角色发展，超越静态记忆
zh.desc: 角色扮演语言代理（RPLA）需要在叙事中动态发展角色，以便角色的价值观和行为随着故事的进展而演变。现有的评估基准主要关注事实回忆，而不是角色的心理轨迹是否一致。我们提出了ArcANE（角色弧意识叙事评估），这是一个自动构建的基准，涵盖17部小说和80个主要角色。通过对角色弧的条件化，ArcANE在所有模型和上下文策略中表现优异，尤其是在源文本未涉及的情境中。

papers.title: TIDE: Proactive Multi-Problem Discovery via Template-Guided Iteration
papers.abstract: Abstract TIDE is a template-guided iterative framework for discovering hidden problems from context, using iterative discovery and thought templates to improve problem identification and resolution across document and code environments.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Agents are widely deployed as assistants over documents, tools, and code. However, they typically act only on explicit user requests, which surface only the problems the user has noticed, while many other important problems coexist, hidden in plain sight, within the broader user context, with their total number unknown in advance. We frame this as the task of discovering multiple hidden problems from context, in which coexisting problems should be uncovered, grounded in supporting evidence, and paired with concrete actions. To this end, we introduce TIDE, a template-guided iterative framework with two complementary mechanisms. Specifically, motivated by the observation that single-pass prediction anchors on the most salient cases and yields generic claims, we propose iterative discovery, which surfaces a small batch of candidates per round while conditioning on what has already been found, so subsequent rounds extend coverage; and thought templates, reusable schemas distilled from previously solved cases that specify what contextual signals to attend to and how to connect them, anchoring each prediction in a recognizable problem class. We validate TIDE on two realistic settings, personal workspaces and software repositories, across four model backbones, showing substantial gains over single-shot and parallel multi-agent baselines on task coverage, identification, and resolution.

ru.title: Итеративное обнаружение скрытых проблем через шаблоны мышления
ru.desc: TIDE — это шаблонный итеративный фреймворк для обнаружения скрытых проблем в контексте документов и кода. Метод использует итеративное открытие проблем, где каждый раунд находит новые кандидаты, обусловленные уже найденными случаями, что позволяет расширить охват. Авторы предлагают использовать шаблоны мышления — переиспользуемые схемы, извлеченные из ранее решённых задач, которые указывают, на какие сигналы контекста обращать внимание. Эксперименты на личных рабочих пространствах и программных репозиториях показывают значительное улучшение по сравнению с однопроходными и параллельными мультиагентными подходами.

en.title: Uncovering Hidden Problems with TIDE: Iterative Discovery and Thought Templates
en.desc: TIDE is a framework designed to uncover hidden problems in documents and code by using an iterative approach and thought templates. It addresses the limitation of traditional assistants that only respond to explicit user requests, which can miss many underlying issues. By employing iterative discovery, TIDE generates a series of problem candidates in rounds, building on previous findings to enhance problem coverage. The use of thought templates helps to focus on relevant contextual signals, ensuring that each identified problem is grounded in evidence and linked to actionable solutions.

zh.title: TIDE：揭示隐藏问题的智能框架
zh.desc: TIDE是一个基于模板的迭代框架，旨在从上下文中发现隐藏的问题。它通过迭代发现和思维模板来提高问题识别和解决的效率，适用于文档和代码环境。该框架能够揭示共存的隐藏问题，并为每个问题提供具体的解决方案。通过在个人工作空间和软件库中的验证，TIDE在任务覆盖率、识别和解决方面表现出显著的提升。

papers.title: AdaPlanBench: Evaluating Adaptive Planning in Large Language Model Agents under World and User Constraints
papers.abstract: Abstract AdaPlanBench presents a dynamic interactive benchmark for evaluating LLM agents' ability to adaptively plan under progressively revealed world and user constraints through multi-turn interactions.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Planning for real-world problems by language models often involves both world and user constraints, which may not be fully specified upfront and are progressively disclosed through interaction. However, existing benchmarks still underexplore adaptive planning under such progressively revealed dual constraints. To address this gap, we introduce AdaPlanBench, a dynamic interactive benchmark for evaluating whether Large Language Model (LLM) agents can adaptively plan and re-plan under progressively revealed world and user constraints. AdaPlanBench is built on 307 household tasks, with a scalable constraint construction pipeline that augments each task with dual constraints. At runtime, agents interact with the environment in a multi-turn protocol where hidden constraints are revealed only when the agent proposes a plan that violates them, requiring iterative plan revision under accumulating feedback. This makes planning challenging, as agents must infer and track constraints from feedback while re-planning effectively. Experiments on ten leading LLMs show that adaptive planning under dual constraints remains challenging, with the best model reaching only 67.75% accuracy. We further observe that performance degrades as more constraints accumulate, with user constraints posing a particularly large challenge and failures often stemming from weaker physical grounding and reduced effectiveness. These results establish AdaPlanBench as a testbed for dual-constrained interactive planning and highlight the challenge of reliable adaptation to dynamically revealed constraints in LLM agents.

ru.title: Адаптивное планирование под неизвестные ограничения: вызов для больших языковых моделей
ru.desc: Статья представляет AdaPlanBench — динамический интерактивный бенчмарк для оценки способности LLM агентов адаптивно планировать под постепенно раскрываемые ограничения окружающего мира и предпочтения пользователя. Бенчмарк построен на 307 бытовых задачах с двойными ограничениями, которые раскрываются в процессе многошагового взаимодействия агента с окружением: скрытые ограничения становятся известны только при нарушении плана. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что даже лучшие LLM достигают только 67,75% точности при адаптивном планировании, при этом производительность снижается с накоплением ограничений. Исследование выявило, что пользовательские ограничения особенно сложны для моделей, а основные ошибки связаны с недостаточным физическим осознанием и неэффективной переплани́ровкой.

en.title: Adaptive Planning Under Revealed Constraints
en.desc: AdaPlanBench is a new benchmark designed to test how well Large Language Model (LLM) agents can adapt their planning strategies when faced with constraints that are revealed over time. It focuses on real-world tasks where both world and user constraints are not fully known at the start and must be discovered through interaction. The benchmark involves a multi-turn process where agents must revise their plans based on feedback about hidden constraints, making the planning process more complex. Experiments show that even the best LLMs struggle with this adaptive planning, especially as the number of constraints increases, highlighting the need for better models that can handle dynamic environments.

zh.title: 动态约束下的自适应规划挑战
zh.desc: AdaPlanBench是一个动态交互基准，用于评估大型语言模型（LLM）代理在逐步揭示的世界和用户约束下的自适应规划能力。该基准基于307个家庭任务，构建了一个可扩展的约束构建管道，为每个任务增加了双重约束。在运行时，代理通过多轮交互与环境互动，只有在代理提出的计划违反约束时，隐藏的约束才会被揭示，这要求代理在不断反馈下进行迭代计划修订。实验结果表明，在双重约束下的自适应规划仍然具有挑战性，最佳模型的准确率仅为67.75%。

papers.title: Reinforcement Learning Elicits Contextual Learning of Unseen Language Translation
papers.abstract: Abstract Reinforcement learning approach enables large language models to translate unseen languages by leveraging in-context linguistic knowledge rather than memorizing specific languages.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Prior work has shown that large language models (LLMs) can translate unseen or low-resource languages by undergoing continued training or even by encoding a grammar book in their context. However, both methods typically overfit specific languages, with limited zero-shot transfer at test time. To translate extremely low-resource languages at scale, we argue that LLMs must acquire the meta-skill of utilizing in-context linguistic knowledge rather than memorizing specific languages. In this paper, we propose a reinforcement learning (RL) approach to unseen language translation given rich linguistic context, using a surface-level translation metric (chrF) as the reward. Empirically, despite the lightweight reward, our RL-trained models effectively extract and apply relevant linguistic information from the provided context, leading to better translations on completely unseen languages than in-context learning or supervised fine-tuning. Our analyses suggest that outcome-based RL can extend beyond conventional reasoning tasks like math and coding to serve as a recipe for language learning from context.

ru.title: Учимся переводить неизвестные языки через контекстное понимание
ru.desc: В статье предлагается подход на основе обучения с подкреплением для перевода невиданных языков большими языковыми моделями. Вместо запоминания конкретных языков, модели обучаются извлекать и применять лингвистическую информацию из контекста, используя метрику chrF в качестве сигнала награды. Экспериментально показано, что RL-обученные модели эффективнее справляются с переводом языков с очень ограниченными ресурсами в сравнении с обучением в контексте и контролируемой дообучением. Результаты демонстрируют, что обучение с подкреплением на основе результатов может быть применено не только к задачам рассуждения, но и к изучению языков из контекста.

en.title: Empowering Language Models with Reinforcement Learning for Unseen Language Translation
en.desc: This paper presents a reinforcement learning (RL) approach to improve the translation of unseen languages using large language models (LLMs). Instead of memorizing specific languages, the models learn to utilize in-context linguistic knowledge, which enhances their ability to translate low-resource languages effectively. The authors demonstrate that using a surface-level translation metric as a reward allows the models to extract relevant linguistic information from context, resulting in superior translations compared to traditional methods. This research suggests that RL can be a powerful tool for language learning, extending its application beyond typical reasoning tasks.

zh.title: 利用上下文知识提升未见语言翻译能力
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了强化学习在大型语言模型（LLMs）翻译未见语言中的应用。研究表明，LLMs可以通过利用上下文中的语言知识，而不是单纯记忆特定语言，来实现更好的翻译效果。作者提出了一种基于强化学习的方法，使用表面翻译指标（chrF）作为奖励，来优化翻译质量。实验结果显示，经过强化学习训练的模型在翻译完全未见的语言时，表现优于传统的上下文学习或监督微调方法。

papers.title: RobotValues: Evaluating Household Robots When Human Values Conflict
papers.abstract: Abstract RobotValues benchmark evaluates household robot planners in value-conflict scenarios, revealing that vision-language models exhibit default value preferences and struggle to override them when instructed to prioritize conflicting values.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct While household robots are often evaluated based on task completion, everyday domestic environments involve value-conflicting situations in which robots are expected to choose actions that prioritize other values than task success, such as human autonomy, efficiency, or social appropriateness. Yet, there are no benchmarks for evaluating robots' value preferences in such scenarios. We introduce RobotValues, a benchmark to evaluate household robot planners in 10K value-conflict scenarios. Each instance consists of a realistic household image with multiple plausible robot actions that prioritize different human values. We construct RobotValues through LLM-assisted scenario generation, stakeholder-grounded value extraction, image generation and automatic quality control. Using RobotValues we evaluate VLMs used in robotics and find that models exhibit default value preferences, including safety and accommodation, while underselecting privacy-prioritizing actions. When the models are instructed to prioritize specific values that conflict with their own preferences, they often fail to override their default actions, choosing incorrect actions for 80% of the time. These findings suggest that household robot evaluation should measure not only task completion or safety compliance, but also whether robots can choose among plausible actions when human values conflict.
affiliations: Graduate School of Data Science, Seoul National University

ru.title: Роботы должны уважать ценности, а не только выполнять задачи
ru.desc: В статье представлен бенчмарк RobotValues для оценки планировщиков домашних роботов в сценариях с конфликтующими ценностями. Исследователи создали набор из 10 тысяч сценариев с реалистичными изображениями кухни, где каждое действие робота отражает разные человеческие ценности. С помощью LLM были сгенерированы сценарии и проведена автоматическая проверка качества. Анализ показал, что видео-языковые модели имеют встроенные предпочтения (безопасность, помощь) и в 80% случаев не могут переопределить свои решения при инструкциях приоритизировать конфликтующие ценности.

en.title: Evaluating Robots Beyond Tasks: The Value Conflict Challenge
en.desc: The paper introduces the RobotValues benchmark, which assesses household robot planners in scenarios where different human values conflict. It highlights that current vision-language models (VLMs) tend to have default value preferences, such as prioritizing safety and accommodation, but struggle to adapt when instructed to prioritize conflicting values like privacy. The benchmark consists of 10,000 scenarios that present realistic household images with various actions that reflect different human values. The findings indicate that robots often fail to override their default preferences, making incorrect choices 80% of the time when faced with value conflicts, suggesting a need for more comprehensive evaluation metrics in robotic systems.

zh.title: 评估家庭机器人价值选择能力的新基准
zh.desc: RobotValues基准测试评估家庭机器人在价值冲突场景中的规划能力。研究发现，视觉语言模型在面对价值冲突时，往往表现出默认的价值偏好，并且在被指示优先考虑冲突价值时，难以覆盖这些偏好。该基准通过生成真实的家庭图像和多种可能的机器人行动，构建了10,000个价值冲突场景。结果表明，家庭机器人评估不仅应关注任务完成或安全合规，还应考虑机器人在面对人类价值冲突时的选择能力。

papers.title: Personal AI Agent for Camera Roll VQA
papers.abstract: Abstract A conversational AI agent is developed for personal camera roll visual question answering, featuring hierarchical memory and specialized tools for navigating large visual datasets with personalized content.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We study the personal camera roll visual question answering setting. In this setting, a conversational AI assistant can access a user's personal camera roll and retrieve relevant photos to answer queries, ranging from simple factual questions (e.g., ``Name of the food I tried yesterday?'') to more open-ended ones (e.g., ``Recommend some dishes I have never eaten before''). Given the vast nature of the personal camera roll (i.e., multiple years, hundreds to thousands of photos), a successful AI assistant needs to understand a long-horizon, highly personalized visual content stream in order to navigate and locate the correct and/or relevant information. To support this, we collect and manually annotate questions that mimic real-world usage. The final dataset, camroll, contains 50 users, 31,476 images, and 2,500 QA pairs. We further design camroll-agent, a conversational AI agent equipped with hierarchical memory and a minimal set of tools for efficient navigation over large, personalized visual memory. Experimental results show that camroll-agent outperforms numerous baselines and methods for long-context understanding AI agents system. Together, the camroll dataset and camroll-agent highlight the gap in AI agents' long-context reasoning: personalized visual memory requires different approaches from standard long-context textual memory, especially when consistency, visual details, and user-specific context are present.

ru.title: Персонализированное понимание визуальной памяти через иерархическое структурирование и долгосрочный контекст
ru.desc: В работе предлагается conversational AI агент для ответа на вопросы о личной фотогалерее пользователя. Агент использует иерархическую память и специализированные инструменты для эффективной навигации по большим наборам персонализированных изображений. Авторы создали датасет камroll с 31 476 изображениями и 2 500 парами вопрос-ответ от 50 пользователей, отражающими реальные сценарии использования. Результаты показывают, что предложенный агент превосходит базовые подходы при работе с долгосрочным контекстом и демонстрирует необходимость специальных методов для персонализированной визуальной памяти, отличающихся от стандартных подходов работы с текстовыми данными.

en.title: Revolutionizing Personal Photo Queries with AI
en.desc: This paper presents a conversational AI agent designed for answering questions about a user's personal camera roll, which is a collection of their photos. The agent utilizes hierarchical memory to efficiently navigate through large datasets of personalized images, allowing it to respond to both specific and open-ended queries. A new dataset called camroll was created, containing thousands of images and annotated questions to train and evaluate the AI's performance. The results demonstrate that the camroll-agent significantly improves long-context understanding in visual memory compared to existing methods, emphasizing the need for tailored approaches in handling personalized visual data.

zh.title: 个性化视觉问答的智能助手
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种用于个人相册视觉问答的对话式人工智能代理。该代理具备层次化记忆和专门工具，能够在庞大的个性化视觉数据集中进行导航。研究表明，成功的AI助手需要理解长期的、个性化的视觉内容流，以便准确定位相关信息。通过收集和手动标注问题，最终构建了包含50个用户、31,476张图片和2,500个问答对的数据集，展示了个性化视觉记忆与标准文本记忆在长上下文推理中的不同需求。

papers.title: Rethinking Continual Experience Internalization for Self-Evolving LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Experience internalization enables continual learning in large language models by converting past interactions into reusable capabilities, with key findings on experience granularity, injection patterns, and internalization regimes for stable learning.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Experience internalization converts contextual experience from past interactions into reusable parametric capability, offering a promising path toward continual learning in large language models (LLMs). While prior work has predominantly focused on single-iteration transfer, we discover that under multi-iteration experience learning, existing methods suffer from a progressive capability collapse rather than compounding improvement. We systematically examine this failure through three vital dimensions of experience internalization: (1) Experience Granularity: We find that principle-level experience is more durable than instance-level experience, as it effectively abstracts transferable strategies away from trajectory-specific details. (2) Experience Injection Pattern: Our analysis reveals that step-wise injection significantly outperforms global injection by aligning experience with intermediate decision states, a property that is critical for long-horizon tool use. (3) Internalization Regime: We demonstrate that off-policy context-distillation on high-quality teacher trajectories provides a substantially more stable training signal than on-policy context-distillation, which is inherently limited by local corrections on student-induced flawed states. Together, these insights yield a simple yet robust recipe for stable and sustainable experience internalization, providing concrete guidance for engineering self-evolving and continually learning LLMs.
affiliations: Gaoling School of Artificial Intelligence, Renmin University of China

ru.title: Стабильная интернализация опыта для континуального обучения языковых моделей
ru.desc: Работа исследует интернализацию опыта в больших языковых моделях (LLM), позволяющую преобразовывать прошлые взаимодействия в переиспользуемые параметрические способности для континуального обучения. Авторы выявили, что при многократном обучении на опыте существующие методы страдают от прогрессивного коллапса способностей вместо накопления улучшений. Исследование систематически анализирует три ключевых аспекта: гранулярность опыта (принципиально-уровневый опыт долговечнее, чем опыт конкретных примеров), паттерны инъекции (пошаговая инъекция эффективнее глобальной) и режимы интернализации (внеполитическая контекстная дистилляция на качественных траекториях учителя стабильнее). Результаты исследования предоставляют практическое руководство для создания саморазвивающихся и континуально обучающихся LLM.

en.title: Transforming Past Interactions into Future Skills for LLMs
en.desc: This paper discusses a method called experience internalization, which helps large language models (LLMs) learn continuously by turning past interactions into reusable skills. The authors found that learning from broader principles (experience granularity) is more effective than focusing on specific instances, as it allows for better generalization. They also discovered that injecting experiences step-by-step is more beneficial than doing it all at once, as it aligns better with the model's decision-making process. Lastly, they show that using high-quality examples from experienced models (off-policy context-distillation) leads to more stable learning compared to relying on the model's own recent experiences (on-policy context-distillation).

zh.title: 经验内化：大型语言模型的持续学习之道
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了如何通过经验内化来实现大型语言模型的持续学习。研究发现，经验的粒度、注入模式和内化机制对学习的稳定性至关重要。具体来说，原则级的经验比实例级的经验更持久，逐步注入的方式优于全局注入。最后，使用高质量教师轨迹的离策略上下文蒸馏能提供更稳定的训练信号，从而促进模型的自我演化和持续学习。

papers.title: LoomVideo: Unifying Multimodal Inputs into Video Generation and Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract LoomVideo presents an efficient 5B-parameter unified architecture for video generation and editing that reduces computational overhead through novel conditioning mechanisms and multi-modal alignment techniques.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Developing unified video generation and editing models capable of interpreting interleaved multimodal inputs is a promising yet challenging frontier field. Existing unified frameworks predominantly rely on massive models (typically 13B parameters or more) and incorporate source video conditions for editing by concatenating sequence tokens. This concatenation inevitably doubles the sequence length, quadrupling the computational complexity of the self-attention mechanism and introducing prohibitive overhead. To address these bottlenecks, we present LoomVideo, a highly efficient 5B-parameter unified architecture for both video generation and editing. LoomVideo replaces the standard text encoder with a Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) and employs Deepstack injection mechanism to align multi-layer MLLM features with the Diffusion Transformer (DiT). Crucially, we introduce a zero-overhead Scale-and-Add conditioning approach for video editing. By scaling and directly adding the clean source video latent to the noised target latent, this elegant design eliminates the need for token concatenation, drastically reducing computational cost while maintaining robust capabilities for complex, non-rigid edits. Furthermore, a Negative Temporal RoPE strategy is seamlessly integrated to handle multiple reference images. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our compact 5B model achieves state-of-the-art or highly competitive performance across comprehensive benchmarks, exhibiting exceptional superiority in e-commerce and fashion generation scenarios. Benefiting from the zero-overhead conditioning mechanism, LoomVideo achieves at least a 5.41x acceleration in inference speed compared to models of similar capabilities, paving the way for highly practical and efficient video foundation models.

ru.title: Эффективная унификация: генерация и редактирование видео без вычислительных потерь
ru.desc: LoomVideo представляет компактную архитектуру с 5 миллиардами параметров для унифицированной генерации и редактирования видео. Модель использует многомодальную большую языковую модель (MLLM) вместо стандартного текстового кодировщика и применяет механизм Deepstack для выравнивания признаков MLLM с диффузионным трансформатором (DiT). Ключевая инновация — подход Scale-and-Add для кондиционирования видео при редактировании, который избегает конкатенации токенов и снижает вычислительные затраты в четыре раза. Модель достигает лучших или конкурентных результатов на бенчмарках и обеспечивает ускорение вывода минимум в 5.41 раза по сравнению с аналогичными моделями.

en.title: Efficient Video Generation and Editing with LoomVideo
en.desc: LoomVideo introduces a compact 5B-parameter architecture for video generation and editing, significantly reducing computational demands. It utilizes innovative conditioning mechanisms and multi-modal alignment techniques to enhance performance without the need for large models. By implementing a zero-overhead Scale-and-Add conditioning approach, LoomVideo avoids the computational burden of token concatenation, allowing for efficient processing of complex edits. The model demonstrates state-of-the-art performance in various benchmarks, particularly in e-commerce and fashion, while achieving a remarkable acceleration in inference speed.

zh.title: 高效视频生成与编辑的未来
zh.desc: LoomVideo是一种高效的5B参数统一架构，专注于视频生成和编辑。它通过新颖的条件机制和多模态对齐技术，显著降低了计算开销。与传统的庞大模型相比，LoomVideo采用了多模态大语言模型，并引入了零开销的Scale-and-Add条件方法，避免了序列连接带来的复杂性。实验表明，该模型在电商和时尚生成场景中表现出色，推理速度比同类模型快至少5.41倍。

papers.title: Unsupervised Skill Discovery for Agentic Data Analysis
papers.abstract: Abstract DataCOPE is an unsupervised framework that discovers reusable data-analysis skills through verifier-guided exploration, improving analytical performance in both report-style and reasoning-style tasks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Inference-time skill augmentation provides a lightweight way to improve data-analytic agents by injecting reusable procedural knowledge without updating model parameters. However, discovering effective skills for data analysis remains challenging, as reliable supervision is expensive and success criteria vary across analytical formats. This raises the key question of how to discover reusable data-analysis skills from unlabeled exploration alone. We propose DataCOPE, an unsupervised verifier-guided skill discovery framework for data-analytic agents. DataCOPE derives verifier signals from the exploration trajectories and uses them to characterize relative quality or aggreement among trajectories. It iteratively coordinates a Data-Analytic Agent for trajectory generation, an Unsupervised Verifier for signal extraction, and a Skill Manager for contrastive skill distillation. For report-style analysis, we instantiate the verifier as an Adaptive Checklist Verifier that derives task-specific criteria, scores reports by verifiable coverage, and iteratively refines the checklist. For reasoning-style analysis, we instantiate it as an Answer Agreement Verifier that groups trajectories by answer agreement and uses self-consistency as an auxiliary signal. We evaluate DataCOPE on report-style analysis from Deep Data Research and reasoning-style analysis from DABStep. Across both settings, DataCOPE consistently improves held-out performance over baselines. Averaged across four model settings, DataCOPE improves the mean score by 9.71% and 32.30% on report-style and reasoning-style tasks respectively.

ru.title: Самообучаемое открытие навыков аналитики данных без разметки
ru.desc: DataCOPE — это фреймворк без учителя, который обнаруживает переиспользуемые навыки анализа данных через управляемое верификатором исследование. Система координирует работу трёх компонентов: агента анализа данных для генерации траекторий, непроверяемого верификатора для извлечения сигналов качества и менеджера навыков для дистилляции навыков через контрастивное обучение. Для отчётного анализа используется адаптивный верификатор чек-листов, а для рассуждений — верификатор согласованности ответов, опирающийся на принцип самосогласованности. Метод показывает улучшение производительности на 9,71% для отчётного анализа и на 32,30% для анализа с рассуждениями по сравнению с базовыми решениями.

en.title: Unlocking Data-Analysis Skills with Unsupervised Learning
en.desc: DataCOPE is an unsupervised framework designed to enhance data-analysis skills by using a verifier-guided exploration method. It allows data-analytic agents to improve their performance in both report-style and reasoning-style tasks without needing to update their model parameters. The framework discovers effective skills from unlabeled data by coordinating a trajectory generation process, extracting signals through an unsupervised verifier, and managing skill distillation. Evaluation results show that DataCOPE significantly boosts performance, achieving improvements of 9.71% and 32.30% in report-style and reasoning-style tasks, respectively.

zh.title: 无监督技能发现，提升数据分析能力
zh.desc: DataCOPE是一个无监督框架，通过验证者引导的探索发现可重用的数据分析技能，从而提高报告式和推理式任务的分析性能。该框架在推理时增强技能，提供了一种轻量级的方法，通过注入可重用的程序知识来改善数据分析代理，而无需更新模型参数。尽管发现有效的数据分析技能仍然具有挑战性，但DataCOPE通过探索轨迹提取验证信号，迭代协调数据分析代理、无监督验证者和技能管理器，从而实现技能的对比蒸馏。实验结果表明，DataCOPE在报告式和推理式分析中均显著提高了性能，平均提升了9.71%和32.30%。

papers.title: Complexity-Balanced Diffusion Splitting
papers.abstract: Abstract Complexity-Balanced Splitting (CBS) allocates generative capacity across specialized sub-networks by partitioning the diffusion timeline based on local complexity measures, improving synthesis quality without increasing inference cost.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Standard continuous-time generative models rely on monolithic architectures that must navigate vastly different signal regimes, from isotropic noise to intricate data distributions. While scaling model capacity improves performance, deploying a massive network uniformly across the entire generative timeline is inherently inefficient. In this work, we propose Complexity-Balanced Splitting (CBS), a principled framework for temporal capacity allocation that distributes the generative workload across multiple specialized sub-networks. Grounded in function approximation theory and de Boor's equidistribution principle, CBS partitions the diffusion timeline into segments of equal approximation burden, allocating more representational capacity to regions where the generative dynamics are more difficult to model. To estimate this local complexity, we introduce two complementary and tractable monitor functions: a spatial measure based on the flow's Dirichlet energy, and a geometric measure based on the acceleration of the sampling trajectories. Using a lightweight auxiliary model to estimate these complexity profiles, our approach eliminates the need for heuristic temporal splits or computationally expensive search procedures. Extensive evaluation across multiple architectures (SiT, JiT, and UNet) and datasets demonstrates that CBS consistently improves synthesis quality without increasing per-step inference cost. In particular, CBS improves FID by ~35% on SiT-XL with CFG relative to naive temporal partitioning. Project page is available at https://noamissachar.github.io/CBS/.

ru.title: Балансировка вычислительных ресурсов по сложности в диффузионных моделях
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод Complexity-Balanced Splitting (CBS) для оптимального распределения вычислительной мощности генеративной модели диффузии во времени. Вместо использования одной монолитной архитектуры на всех этапах генерации, метод разбивает временную шкалу диффузии на сегменты на основе локальных мер сложности сигнала. Для оценки сложности в каждый момент времени используются два дополнительных монитор-функции: пространственная мера на основе энергии Дирихле потока и геометрическая мера на основе ускорения траекторий сэмплирования. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что CBS улучшает качество синтеза (на ~35% по метрике FID) без увеличения вычислительных затрат на шаг.

en.title: Efficient Generative Modeling with Complexity-Balanced Splitting
en.desc: The paper introduces Complexity-Balanced Splitting (CBS), a method that enhances generative model performance by efficiently distributing the workload across specialized sub-networks. It does this by analyzing the complexity of different segments in the diffusion timeline, allowing more resources to be allocated where the data is harder to model. CBS uses two innovative monitor functions to assess local complexity, which helps in making informed decisions about how to split the generative tasks. The results show that CBS significantly improves synthesis quality, achieving a notable reduction in FID scores without increasing inference costs.

zh.title: 复杂性平衡，提升生成质量！
zh.desc: 复杂性平衡分割（CBS）是一种通过根据局部复杂性度量划分扩散时间线来分配生成能力的框架。它将生成工作负载分配到多个专门的子网络中，从而提高合成质量而不增加推理成本。CBS基于函数逼近理论和de Boor的均匀分布原理，将扩散时间线划分为相等的逼近负担段，并在生成动态更难建模的区域分配更多的表示能力。通过使用轻量级辅助模型来估计复杂性特征，CBS消除了对启发式时间划分或计算密集型搜索过程的需求。

papers.title: The Shadow Price of Reasoning: Economic Perspective on Optimal Budget Allocation for LLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract Inference-time scaling is enhanced through constrained optimization that allocates computational resources based on economic principles, improving performance in resource-constrained environments.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Inference-time scaling has emerged as a critical avenue for enhancing Large Language Models' performance, yet real-world deployment is constrained by strict computational budgets. In this work, we formulate inference budget allocation as a global constrained optimization problem governed by economic principles. By modeling per-query reasoning utility with a shifted-surge function, we derive an optimal allocation policy based on a global shadow price that equilibrates marginal utility under resource scarcity. Based on this theory, we propose Constrained Latent-utility Equilibrium Allocation for Reasoning (CLEAR). It performs rational abandonment and reallocates resources from insolvent queries to solvable queries near their emergence thresholds.   Extensive experiments on several reasoning tasks with different traffic streams demonstrate that CLEAR significantly improves the Pareto frontier of total token cost versus mean accuracy. In resource-scarce regimes, CLEAR achieves up to a 3x improvement in global accuracy compared to uniform allocation.

ru.title: Умное распределение вычислений: экономика инференса LLM
ru.desc: В работе предлагается метод оптимального распределения вычислительных ресурсов во время инференса больших языковых моделей, основанный на экономических принципах. Авторы формулируют задачу как проблему глобальной условной оптимизации и моделируют полезность рассуждений для каждого запроса с помощью специальной функции. На основе теории равновесия они разрабатывают алгоритм CLEAR, который перераспределяет вычислительные ресурсы, отказываясь от неперспективных запросов в пользу близких к решению. Эксперименты показывают, что предложенный подход улучшает соотношение качества и затрат токенов в 3 раза по сравнению с равномерным распределением.

en.title: Optimizing Resource Allocation for Enhanced Model Performance
en.desc: This paper presents a method called CLEAR, which optimizes how computational resources are allocated during inference for Large Language Models. It treats the allocation problem as a constrained optimization issue, using economic principles to maximize performance under limited resources. By modeling the utility of reasoning for each query, CLEAR reallocates resources from less solvable queries to those that are more likely to succeed. The results show that CLEAR can significantly enhance accuracy while reducing costs, achieving up to three times better performance in resource-constrained situations compared to traditional methods.

zh.title: 优化推理资源分配，提升模型性能
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种基于经济原则的约束优化方法，以提高推理时间的资源分配效率。我们将推理预算分配建模为一个全局约束优化问题，并使用移位激增函数来描述每个查询的推理效用。通过这一理论，我们提出了CLEAR（约束潜在效用均衡分配），它能够在资源稀缺的情况下，合理放弃无效查询并将资源重新分配给可解决的查询。实验结果表明，CLEAR在多种推理任务中显著提高了总令牌成本与平均准确率的帕累托前沿。

papers.title: World-Language-Action Model for Unified World Modeling, Language Reasoning, and Action Synthesis
papers.abstract: Abstract World-language-action models combine textual instruction processing with robot state prediction through an autoregressive transformer backbone, enabling efficient long-horizon task execution and cross-embodiment learning.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We propose world-language-action (WLA) models as a new class of embodied foundation models. WLA takes textual instructions, images, and robot states as inputs to jointly predict textual subtasks, subgoal images, and robot actions, conjoining the world modeling interface to learn from extensive egocentric videos as in the world-action model (WAM) and the language reasoning capacities to solve complex long-horizon tasks as in vision-language-action (VLA) models. At the core of WLA lies an autoregressive (AR) Transformer backbone, instead of a bidirectional diffusion Transformer as in WAMs, to predict the next state, comprising the semantic-level textual intention and complementary fine-grained physical dynamics. The physical dynamics are supervised by the world modeling objective based on a dedicated World Expert, and are leveraged to ease the characterization of the state-action correlation for the Action Expert. WLA leverages meta-queries to make the world prediction implicitly impact the action generation so that the former can be disabled during inference. The world prediction can also be activated to enable test-time scaling for improved robot control. Our WLA-0 prototype, with 2B active parameters, achieves 40 ms per inference on an NVIDIA RTX 5090. Evaluations across simulated and real-world environments demonstrate that WLA-0 achieves state-of-the-art multi-task and long-horizon learning abilities, e.g., 92.94\% success rate on RoboTwin2.0 Clean and 56.5\% success rate on RMBench. WLA-0 also holds the promise to learn novel tasks directly from cross-embodiment robot videos without action annotations.

ru.title: Единая модель для понимания языка, прогнозирования мира и управления роботом
ru.desc: В статье предложены модели World-Language-Action (WLA) — новый класс встроенных foundation-моделей, которые принимают текстовые инструкции, изображения и состояния робота для совместного предсказания подзадач, промежуточных целей и действий робота. Архитектура основана на autoregressive Transformer, который предсказывает следующее состояние как на семантическом уровне (текстовые намерения), так и на уровне физической динамики. Модель использует специализированные эксперты для world modeling и action generation, с возможностью отключения предсказания мира во время инференса и использования test-time scaling для улучшения контроля. Прототип WLA-0 с 2 млрд активных параметров демонстрирует state-of-the-art результаты в многозадачном обучении и решении долгосрочных задач, достигая 92.94% успеха на RoboTwin2.0 и способности обучаться на видео разных типов роботов без аннотаций действий.

en.title: Empowering Robots with Language and Action Predictions
en.desc: The paper introduces world-language-action (WLA) models, which integrate text instructions, images, and robot states to predict actions and subgoals for robots. Utilizing an autoregressive transformer backbone, WLA models enhance long-horizon task execution by combining language reasoning with physical dynamics. The model learns from egocentric videos, allowing it to generalize across different robot embodiments without needing explicit action annotations. Evaluations show that WLA-0 achieves high success rates in both simulated and real-world tasks, demonstrating its effectiveness in multi-task learning and robot control.

zh.title: 世界语言行动模型：智能机器人新纪元
zh.desc: 世界语言行动模型（WLA）是一种新型的具身基础模型，它结合了文本指令处理和机器人状态预测。WLA模型使用自回归变换器（AR Transformer）作为核心，能够有效地执行长时间任务并进行跨体现学习。该模型通过输入文本指令、图像和机器人状态，联合预测文本子任务、子目标图像和机器人动作，从而实现复杂任务的解决。WLA-0原型在模拟和真实环境中表现出色，展示了其在多任务和长时间学习能力上的先进性。

papers.title: Imagine Before You Predict: Interleaved Latent Visual Reasoning for Video Event Prediction
papers.abstract: Abstract Future-L1, an interleaved latent visual reasoning framework, improves video event prediction by maintaining visual semantics in latent space during autoregressive decoding, achieving state-of-the-art results on FutureBench and TwiFF-Bench benchmarks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Video event prediction (VEP) requires models to infer unobserved future states from partial video evidence. Existing video MLLMs usually verbalize intermediate future reasoning in text space: once visual evidence is verbalized, fine-grained motion, geometry, and interaction cues can be lost, leading to plausible but visually ungrounded hallucinations. We introduce Future-L1, an interleaved latent visual reasoning framework that lets an MLLM alternate between language tokens and continuous latent visual spans during autoregressive decoding. To train this capability, we construct Future-L1-50K by selecting examples where future visual hints help prediction and align latent states to future-frame embeddings, then further optimize sampled latent trajectories with LA-DAPO, a latent-aware RL objective with outcome-contrastive and temporal-diversity rewards. Future-L1 achieves new state-of-the-art results on both benchmarks: on FutureBench, it improves Qwen3-VL-8B from 61.0 to 85.4 and exceeds the previous best Video-CoE by 10.4 points; on TwiFF-Bench, it improves the average score from 2.44 to 3.04. These results suggest that future-oriented video reasoning benefits from preserving intermediate visual semantics in latent space rather than translating every reasoning step into text.
affiliations: University of Electronic Science and Technology of China
University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Предсказание будущего через скрытые визуальные рассуждения вместо текстовой вербализации
ru.desc: Future-L1 — это фреймворк для предсказания событий в видео, который использует интерпретируемое рассуждение в скрытом пространстве вместо вербализации всех промежуточных шагов в текст. Модель чередует языковые токены и непрерывные визуальные представления во время авторегрессивного декодирования, сохраняя точные движения, геометрию и взаимодействия. Для обучения этой способности авторы создали датасет Future-L1-50K и применили LA-DAPO — объективную функцию усиленного обучения с контрастивными и временными вознаграждениями. Фреймворк достигает новых результатов на бенчмарках FutureBench и TwiFF-Bench, значительно превосходя предыдущие подходы.

en.title: Future-L1: Enhancing Video Predictions with Visual Semantics
en.desc: The paper introduces Future-L1, a novel framework for video event prediction that enhances the model's ability to maintain visual information during the decoding process. By interleaving language tokens with continuous latent visual representations, Future-L1 prevents the loss of crucial visual details that often occurs when reasoning is expressed solely in text. The framework is trained using a specialized dataset, Future-L1-50K, which focuses on examples where visual cues are essential for accurate predictions. As a result, Future-L1 achieves state-of-the-art performance on key benchmarks, demonstrating that preserving visual semantics in latent space significantly improves future video reasoning.

zh.title: 未来导向的视频推理新突破
zh.desc: Future-L1是一种交错的潜在视觉推理框架，通过在自回归解码过程中保持潜在空间中的视觉语义，提升了视频事件预测的效果。该方法解决了现有视频多模态语言模型在中间推理时可能丢失细致的运动、几何和交互线索的问题。Future-L1通过在语言标记和连续潜在视觉片段之间交替，增强了模型的推理能力。实验结果表明，Future-L1在FutureBench和TwiFF-Bench基准测试中均取得了新的最佳成绩，证明了在潜在空间中保留中间视觉语义对未来导向的视频推理的益处。

papers.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
papers.abstract: Abstract MLEvolve is an LLM-based multi-agent framework that enables long-horizon machine learning algorithm discovery through improved search mechanisms, memory systems, and adaptive coding strategies.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly applied to long-horizon tasks such as scientific discovery and machine learning engineering (MLE), where sustained self-evolution becomes a key capability. However, existing MLE agents suffer from inter-branch information isolation, memoryless search, and lack of hierarchical control, which together hinder long-horizon optimization. We present MLEvolve, an LLM-based self-evolving multi-agent framework for end-to-end machine learning algorithm discovery. By extending tree search to Progressive MCGS, MLEvolve enables cross-branch information flow through graph-based reference edges and gradually shifts the search from broad exploration to focused exploitation with an entropy-inspired progressive schedule. To allow the agent to evolve with accumulated experience, we introduce Retrospective Memory, which combines a cold-start domain knowledge base with a dynamic global memory for task-specific experience retrieval and reuse. For stable long-horizon iteration, we further decouple strategic planning from code generation with adaptive coding modes. Evaluation on MLE-Bench shows that MLEvolve achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple dimensions including average medal rate and valid submission rate under a 12-hour budget (half the standard runtime). Moreover, MLEvolve also outperforms specialized algorithm discovery methods including AlphaEvolve on mathematical algorithm optimization tasks, demonstrating strong cross-domain generalization. Our code is available at https://github.com/InternScience/MLEvolve.

ru.title: Самоэволюционирующийся агент для открытия алгоритмов машинного обучения
ru.desc: MLEvolve — это многоагентный фреймворк на основе большой языковой модели для автоматического открытия алгоритмов машинного обучения на протяжении длительных горизонтов оптимизации. Система использует расширенный поиск на дереве (Progressive MCGS) с графовыми связями для обмена информацией между ветвями поиска и постепенного перехода от исследования к эксплуатации. Ретроспективная память объединяет базу знаний предметной области с динамической памятью для накопления и переиспользования опыта при решении задач. Фреймворк демонстрирует передовые результаты на бенчмарке MLE-Bench и превосходит специализированные методы поиска алгоритмов, включая AlphaEvolve.

en.title: Revolutionizing Algorithm Discovery with Self-Evolving Agents
en.desc: MLEvolve is a framework that uses large language models (LLMs) to help discover machine learning algorithms over long periods. It addresses issues like information isolation and memory limitations in existing agents by allowing them to share knowledge and learn from past experiences. The framework employs a unique search strategy that balances exploration and exploitation, enhancing the efficiency of the algorithm discovery process. Evaluations show that MLEvolve outperforms traditional methods, achieving better results in less time across various tasks.

zh.title: MLEvolve：自我演化的机器学习算法发现框架
zh.desc: MLEvolve是一个基于大型语言模型（LLM）的多智能体框架，旨在通过改进的搜索机制、记忆系统和自适应编码策略，实现长期机器学习算法的发现。该框架解决了现有机器学习工程（MLE）智能体在信息隔离、无记忆搜索和缺乏层次控制等方面的问题，从而促进了长期优化。MLEvolve通过扩展树搜索到渐进式多分支图搜索（MCGS），实现了跨分支的信息流动，并采用熵启发的渐进调度，逐步将搜索从广泛探索转向集中利用。通过引入回顾性记忆，MLEvolve能够利用积累的经验进行自我演化，显著提升了在多维度上的性能表现。

papers.title: EvoDS: Self-Evolving Autonomous Data Science Agent with Skill Learning and Context Management
papers.abstract: Abstract EvoDS introduces a self-evolving autonomous data science agent that enhances its capabilities through skill acquisition and adaptive context management via reinforcement learning.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Recent progress in Large Language Model (LLM) agents has enabled promising advances in automated data science. However, existing approaches remain fundamentally limited by their static action sets and lack of principled long-horizon context management, hindering their ability to accumulate reusable experience across tasks and operate reliably in multi-stage, iterative data science pipelines. To address these challenges, we introduce EvoDS, a self-evolving autonomous data science agent that learns to expand its skills and adaptively managing long-term context through agentic reinforcement learning. Specifically, EvoDS introduces two key strategies: (1) Autonomous Skill Acquisition (ASA) mechanism, which enables agents to synthesize, validate, and reuse executable skills; and (2) Adaptive Context Compression (ACC) strategy, which treats context management as a learned control problem rather than passive truncation. These strategies are orchestrated within a two-stage multi-agent training scheme, enabling EvoDS to autonomously improve over time. Theoretically, we prove that EvoDS's hierarchical design reduces tool-selection error, and its optimization objective aligns with an information bottleneck principle, ensuring efficient context use. Empirically, EvoDS outperforms state-of-the-art open-source data science agents by an average of 28.9% across four diverse benchmarks while eliminating out-of-token failures. Our code and data are available at https://github.com/usail-hkust/EvoDS.
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou)

ru.title: Самообучающийся агент, который растёт вместе с задачами
ru.desc: EvoDS — это автономный агент на основе языковой модели, который самостоятельно развивается и улучшает свои способности в решении задач анализа данных. Агент использует механизм автономного приобретения навыков, позволяющий ему создавать, проверять и переиспользовать выполняемые скрипты и функции. Для эффективного управления контекстом в многоэтапных конвейерах EvoDS применяет адаптивное сжатие контекста, рассматривая его как управляемую задачу, а не простое усечение. Система обучается с помощью многоагентного обучения с подкреплением, что позволяет ей накапливать переиспользуемый опыт и превосходить существующие системы на 28,9% по среднему показателю.

en.title: EvoDS: The Future of Self-Evolving Data Science Agents
en.desc: EvoDS is a self-evolving autonomous data science agent that improves its skills and manages context using reinforcement learning. It addresses limitations of existing models by introducing Autonomous Skill Acquisition (ASA) for skill synthesis and validation, and Adaptive Context Compression (ACC) for better context management. This allows EvoDS to learn from past experiences and operate effectively in complex data science tasks. The agent has shown to outperform current leading models by nearly 29% across various benchmarks, demonstrating its advanced capabilities in automated data science.

zh.title: 自我进化的数据科学代理EvoDS
zh.desc: EvoDS是一个自我进化的自主数据科学代理，通过强化学习增强其能力。它引入了自主技能获取机制和自适应上下文压缩策略，使得代理能够合成、验证和重用可执行技能，同时有效管理长期上下文。EvoDS在多阶段的数据科学流程中表现出色，能够在不同任务中积累可重用的经验。实验结果显示，EvoDS在四个基准测试中平均超越了最先进的开源数据科学代理28.9%。

papers.title: Meta-Cognitive Memory Policy Optimization for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Memory-augmented language models struggle with long-horizon tasks due to information loss in recursive summaries, but a new method using belief entropy and metacognitive policy optimization improves performance by focusing on memory quality rather than just outcome success.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Memory-augmented LLM agents tackle complex long-horizon tasks by recursively summarizing interaction trajectories into compact memory. However, existing approaches typically train these memory policies using outcome-based reinforcement learning, failing to localize where intermediate memory quality degrades. As interactions unfold, ambiguous recursive summaries progressively discard task-relevant information and introduce semantic noise. This exacerbates belief deviation, obscuring the agent's estimate of the latent task state and ultimately derailing long-horizon reasoning. We therefore argue that memory optimization should focus not merely on trajectory-level success, but on the clarity of the belief induced by intermediate summaries. To this end, we introduce Belief Entropy, a self-supervised proxy that probes how uncertain the model remains about the latent task state given its current memory. Based on this proxy, we propose Metacognitive Memory Policy Optimization (MMPO). Instead of relying only on sparse outcome-based signals, MMPO provides fine-grained, memory-specific supervision via explicitly penalizing summaries that induce high epistemic uncertainty. Experiments show that MMPO consistently outperforms existing methods on diverse long-horizon tasks, maintaining 97.1% performance even when scaled to 1.75M-token contexts.
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: От исхода к осознанию: оптимизация качества памяти в агентах LLM
ru.desc: Статья рассматривает проблему потери информации в памяти агентов на основе больших языковых моделей при решении длительных задач. Авторы предлагают новый метод, использующий энтропию убеждений для оценки качества промежуточных резюме взаимодействий. Они разработали Metacognitive Memory Policy Optimization (MMPO) - метод оптимизации политики памяти на основе самоконтроля, который обеспечивает детальную оптимизацию качества памяти вместо фокуса только на успехе конечного результата. Эксперименты показывают, что MMPO превосходит существующие методы на задачах с длинными горизонтами, сохраняя высокую производительность даже при масштабировании до 1.75M токенов.

en.title: Enhancing Memory Quality for Long-Horizon Tasks in Language Models
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges faced by memory-augmented language models in handling long-horizon tasks, particularly the loss of important information during recursive summarization. The authors propose a new approach called Metacognitive Memory Policy Optimization (MMPO), which emphasizes the quality of memory rather than just the success of outcomes. By introducing the concept of Belief Entropy, they measure the uncertainty in the model's understanding of the task state based on its memory. The results demonstrate that MMPO significantly improves performance on long-horizon tasks by ensuring clearer and more reliable memory summaries.

zh.title: 优化记忆质量，提升长时间任务表现
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了增强记忆的语言模型在处理长时间任务时面临的信息丢失问题。研究提出了一种新的方法，利用信念熵和元认知策略优化来提高模型的性能，重点关注记忆的质量而不仅仅是结果的成功。通过引入信念熵，模型能够更好地评估其对潜在任务状态的信心，从而优化中间摘要的清晰度。实验结果表明，该方法在多种长时间任务上表现优异，能够在大规模上下文中保持高达97.1%的性能。

papers.title: Towards Truly Multilingual ASR: Generalizing Code-Switching ASR to Unseen Language Pairs
papers.abstract: Abstract Code-switching automatic speech recognition models show limited generalization across unseen language pairs despite attempts at model merging and domain generalization techniques.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) has become a key technology for human--AI interaction. However, code-switching ASR (CS-ASR) remains particularly challenging due to the severe scarcity of multilingual CS speech resources across diverse language pairs. Existing approaches primarily improve CS-ASR performance through synthetic CS speech generation or pair-specific fine-tuning on limited bilingual datasets. Nevertheless, these approaches face an inherent scalability limitation, as support for CS must be developed separately for language pairs whose number grows combinatorially with the number of supported languages. In this work, we investigate whether CS capabilities learned from a limited set of seen language pairs can generalize to unseen language pairs through model merging and domain generalization methods. Our experiments show that merged bilingual CS-ASR models modestly generalize to unseen language pairs, suggesting limited transfer of bilingual CS capabilities across language pairs.
affiliations: College of Computing, Georgia Institute of Technology, GA, USA
Department of Artificial Intelligence, University of Seoul, Seoul, Republic of Korea
Theta One Korea, Seoul, Republic of Korea

ru.title: Пределы обобщения в распознавании речи с кодовым переключением
ru.desc: В статье изучается проблема автоматического распознавания речи с кодовым переключением между языками (CS-ASR). Авторы исследуют, могут ли модели, обученные на ограниченном наборе пар языков, обобщать полученные знания на неизвестные пары языков, используя методы слияния моделей и обобщения на новые домены. Результаты экспериментов показывают, что объединённые двуязычные CS-ASR модели демонстрируют лишь скромное обобщение на новые пары языков, указывая на ограниченную передачу двуязычных возможностей. Исследование выявляет фундаментальный вызов масштабируемости при поддержке кодового переключения для растущего числа языковых пар.

en.title: Unlocking Code-Switching: Limited Generalization in ASR Models
en.desc: This paper explores the challenges of code-switching automatic speech recognition (CS-ASR), which struggles to generalize across different language pairs. The authors investigate whether models trained on a few language pairs can effectively transfer their capabilities to new, unseen pairs using techniques like model merging and domain generalization. They find that while there is some modest generalization, the transfer of bilingual CS capabilities is limited. This highlights the need for more scalable solutions in developing CS-ASR systems for a growing number of language combinations.

zh.title: 提升代码切换语音识别的泛化能力
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了代码切换自动语音识别（CS-ASR）模型在未见语言对上的泛化能力。尽管通过模型合并和领域泛化技术进行尝试，现有模型在不同语言对之间的表现仍然有限。研究表明，合并的双语CS-ASR模型在未见语言对上有一定的泛化能力，但这种能力的转移是有限的。作者的工作强调了在多语言环境中开发更具可扩展性的CS-ASR模型的重要性。

papers.title: Discrete-WAM: Unified Discrete Vision-Action Token Editing for World-Policy Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract Discrete-WAM introduces a unified discrete latent vision-action world policy that enables compositional causal reasoning and counterfactual reasoning in autonomous driving through aligned discrete tokens and a shared discrete diffusion framework.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Autonomous driving requires reasoning about how ego actions shape the evolution of the surrounding world. However, most end-to-end methods rely on direct state-to-action mappings, capturing correlations without explicitly modeling action-conditioned dynamics. Conversely, continuous-latent world models often lack compositional structure for causal reasoning across counterfactual futures. We introduce Discrete-WAM, a unified latent vision-action world policy that represents future visual states and ego actions as aligned discrete tokens, enabling compositional causal reasoning across alternative futures. Built upon this unified discrete alignment, Discrete-WAM establishes a shared discrete diffusion framework with unified generative tasks, jointly formulating world modeling, world-action policy, and hierarchical decision-enabled policy, supporting compositional generalization across diverse driving scenarios. Experiments on large-scale autonomous-driving benchmarks show that Discrete-WAM achieves competitive performance while supporting controllable generation and counterfactual reasoning, offering a principled path toward more reliable decision-making.

ru.title: Дискретные токены для причинного рассуждения в автономном вождении
ru.desc: Discrete-WAM представляет единую модель мировой политики на основе дискретных латентных представлений для автономного вождения, которая объединяет визуальные состояния и действия в выровненные дискретные токены. Метод использует общую diffusion модель для совместного формирования трёх задач: моделирования окружающего мира, политики взаимодействия с миром и иерархической политики принятия решений. Такой подход обеспечивает композиционное причинно-следственное рассуждение и поддержку контрфактических сценариев, позволяя автономному агенту явно моделировать, как его действия влияют на эволюцию окружающей среды. Экспериментальные результаты на крупномасштабных бенчмарках показывают конкурентоспособную производительность при одновременной поддержке управляемой генерации и надёжного принятия решений.

en.title: Empowering Autonomous Driving with Compositional Causal Reasoning
en.desc: The paper presents Discrete-WAM, a novel approach for autonomous driving that integrates discrete latent representations for vision and action. This method allows for compositional causal reasoning, enabling the system to understand how its actions affect the environment and predict various future scenarios. Unlike traditional methods that focus solely on state-to-action mappings, Discrete-WAM utilizes a shared discrete diffusion framework to model the dynamics of the world and the actions taken. The results demonstrate that Discrete-WAM not only performs competitively on benchmarks but also enhances the ability to generate controlled and counterfactual scenarios, improving decision-making reliability.

zh.title: 离散世界模型：自动驾驶的因果推理新路径
zh.desc: Discrete-WAM是一种统一的离散潜在视觉-行动世界策略，能够在自动驾驶中实现组合因果推理和反事实推理。它通过对齐的离散标记和共享的离散扩散框架来表示未来的视觉状态和自我行动。与传统的端到端方法不同，Discrete-WAM不仅捕捉状态与行动之间的相关性，还明确建模了基于行动的动态。实验表明，Discrete-WAM在大型自动驾驶基准测试中表现出色，支持可控生成和反事实推理，为更可靠的决策提供了原则性路径。

papers.title: Latent Reasoning with Normalizing Flows
papers.abstract: Abstract Latent reasoning framework using normalizing flows preserves autoregressive generation advantages while enabling efficient, probabilistic intermediate computation in large language models.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Large language models often improve reasoning by generating explicit chain-of-thought (CoT), demonstrating the importance of intermediate computation. However, textual CoT forces this computation through a discrete, serial, and communication-oriented token stream: each reasoning step must be verbalized before the model can proceed, even when the underlying update is semantic, uncertain, or only partially formed. Latent reasoning offers a higher-bandwidth alternative by performing intermediate computation in compact continuous states before committing to text. Yet existing latent-reasoning methods often sacrifice key advantages that make CoT effective in autoregressive language models, including native left-to-right generation, probabilistic sampling, compatibility with KV-cache decoding, and tractable likelihood estimation. We propose NF-CoT, a latent reasoning framework that preserves these advantages by modeling continuous thoughts with normalizing flows. NF-CoT instantiates a TARFlow-style normalizing flow inside the LLM backbone, defining a tractable probability model over compact continuous thoughts distilled from explicit CoT. Continuous-thought positions are generated by an NF head, while text positions are generated by the standard LM head within the same causal stream. This design provides exact likelihoods for latent thoughts, enables probabilistic left-to-right decoding with the original KV cache, and supports direct policy-gradient optimization in the latent reasoning space. On code-generation benchmarks, NF-CoT improves pass rates over explicit-CoT and prior latent-reasoning baselines while substantially reducing intermediate-reasoning cost.

ru.title: Скрытые мысли через нормализующие потоки: эффективное рассуждение без потери качества генерации
ru.desc: В работе предложена NF-CoT — новая архитектура для латентного рассуждения в больших языковых моделях, которая использует нормализующие потоки для моделирования промежуточных мыслей в непрерывном пространстве. В отличие от явной цепочки рассуждений, где каждый шаг должен быть переведён в токены, этот подход позволяет выполнять вычисления в компактном непрерывном представлении, что экономит вычислительные ресурсы. Ключевое преимущество метода состоит в том, что он сохраняет все достоинства автарегрессивной генерации: левосторонний порядок генерации, вероятностное семплирование, совместимость с KV-кешем и возможность вычисления точного правдоподобия. На бенчмарках для генерации кода NF-CoT показывает улучшение показателей успешности по сравнению с явной цепочкой рассуждений и предыдущими методами латентного рассуждения при значительном снижении затрат на промежуточные вычисления.

en.title: Enhancing Reasoning in Language Models with NF-CoT
en.desc: This paper introduces NF-CoT, a novel latent reasoning framework that enhances large language models by integrating normalizing flows. It allows for efficient intermediate computations in a continuous state, rather than relying solely on discrete text generation. By preserving the advantages of autoregressive models, such as left-to-right generation and probabilistic sampling, NF-CoT improves reasoning capabilities without sacrificing performance. The framework demonstrates significant improvements in code generation tasks, achieving higher pass rates while reducing the costs associated with intermediate reasoning steps.

zh.title: 潜在推理的新突破：NF-CoT框架
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种名为NF-CoT的潜在推理框架，利用归一化流技术来保留自回归生成的优势，同时实现高效的概率中间计算。传统的链式思维（CoT）需要将每一步推理转化为文本，限制了计算的灵活性和效率。NF-CoT通过在紧凑的连续状态中进行中间计算，避免了这种限制，并且能够与现有的语言模型解码机制兼容。实验结果表明，NF-CoT在代码生成基准测试中表现优于传统的链式思维和其他潜在推理方法，同时显著降低了中间推理的成本。

papers.title: OPRD: On-Policy Representation Distillation
papers.abstract: Abstract On-Policy Representation Distillation (OPRD) improves upon traditional on-policy distillation by aligning student and teacher representations in hidden-state space rather than just output space, resulting in reduced variance and improved training efficiency.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct On-policy distillation (OPD) supervises the student only in output space by matching next-token probabilities. This output-only paradigm has two limits: (1) sampling variance from Monte Carlo KL estimates over large vocabularies (e.g., Qwen's ~150k tokens) persists throughout training, and (2) it treats the teacher as a black-box, discarding all intermediate hidden states after the LM head. We propose On-Policy Representation Distillation (OPRD), which lifts distillation into hidden-state space by aligning student and teacher representations across selected layers on the same rollouts, bypassing the LM head entirely. Theoretically, OPRD eliminates sampling variance and provides richer per-layer structural information. Empirically, OPRD closes the student-teacher gap on AIME 2024/2025 and AIMO, while output-space OPD baselines plateau below the teacher. OPRD also trains 1.44x faster and uses 54% less memory than top-k OPD. Code: https://github.com/ShenzhiYang2000/OPRD.

ru.title: Дистиллирование в пространстве скрытых представлений вместо выходных вероятностей
ru.desc: В работе предлагается метод On-Policy Representation Distillation (OPRD), который улучшает традиционное дистиллирование моделей путём выравнивания представлений студента и учителя в пространстве скрытых состояний, а не только в выходном пространстве. Основная проблема классического дистиллирования заключается в высокой дисперсии при оценке KL-дивергенции на больших словарях и потере информации из промежуточных слоёв нейронной сети. Предлагаемый подход OPRD устраняет эти проблемы, выравнивая скрытые представления на одних и тех же выборках данных без использования головки языковой модели. Эмпирические результаты показывают, что OPRD значительно ускоряет обучение (в 1.44 раза), снижает потребление памяти на 54% и улучшает качество на сложных задачах математического рассуждения.

en.title: Aligning Hidden States for Efficient Model Training
en.desc: On-Policy Representation Distillation (OPRD) enhances the process of training a student model by aligning its hidden-state representations with those of a teacher model, rather than just focusing on the output predictions. This method addresses the limitations of traditional on-policy distillation, which only matches output probabilities and suffers from high variance due to large vocabularies. By utilizing hidden states, OPRD reduces sampling variance and captures more detailed structural information from the teacher model. As a result, OPRD not only improves training efficiency, achieving faster training times and lower memory usage, but also significantly narrows the performance gap between the student and teacher models.

zh.title: 提升蒸馏效率，优化模型训练
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的方法，称为在线策略表示蒸馏（OPRD），旨在改进传统的在线策略蒸馏。与仅在输出空间进行匹配的传统方法不同，OPRD通过对齐学生和教师在隐藏状态空间的表示来提高训练效率。该方法消除了采样方差，并提供了更丰富的每层结构信息。实验结果表明，OPRD在多个基准测试中表现优于传统方法，训练速度更快，内存使用更少。

papers.title: Flash-WAM: Modality-Aware Distillation for World Action Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Flash-WAM introduces a modality-aware step-distillation framework for world-action models that achieves real-time inference by adapting consistency functions to different noise regimes in video and action streams.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct World-action models (WAMs) jointly generate future video and robot actions through iterative diffusion, achieving strong performance on manipulation benchmarks but requiring tens of denoising steps, a cost that precludes real-time control. Step distillation has emerged as the natural remedy, but off-the-shelf methods break down in the joint video-action setting because video and action streams use different SNR-shifted noise schedules and reach training with substantially different marginal noise distributions, an asymmetry that single-modality distillation methods cannot accommodate. We introduce Flash-WAM, a modality-aware step-distillation framework inspired by consistency distillation that selects the consistency function for each modality to match its noise regime: a linear-gradient-scaling parametrization for the action stream's low-noise regime, paired with a variance-preserving parametrization for the video stream's high-noise regime, grounded in a structural analysis of the consistency-function family that characterizes the achievable gradient scaling under the consistency boundary condition. Instantiated on LingBot-VA, Flash-WAM compresses inference to a single step in each modality. On RoboTwin 2.0, this reduces per-chunk latency from 8.1 seconds to 348 ms on NVIDIA L40S, a 23{times} speedup that enables real-time inference. Flash-WAM preserves task success on simulation benchmarks (85.5% RoboTwin 2.0, 95.7% LIBERO) and substantially recovers real-world performance (60% average on a Unitree G1 humanoid robot), while naive consistency distillation drops to 24% at the same step budget.

ru.title: Быстрые мировые модели действий через интеллектуальную дистилляцию
ru.desc: Flash-WAM представляет собой фреймворк для пошагового дистилляции моделей мировых действий, который позволяет достичь инференса в реальном времени путём адаптации функций консистентности к различным режимам шума в видео и потоках действий. Основная проблема заключается в том, что видео и действия используют разные расписания шума и имеют существенно отличающиеся маргинальные распределения шума, с чем не справляются стандартные методы дистилляции. Авторы предлагают модально-осведомленный подход, выбирая различные параметризации консистентности для каждой модальности: линейное масштабирование градиента для низкошумного режима действий и сохраняющую дисперсию параметризацию для высокошумного режима видео. На практике Flash-WAM сокращает задержку инференса с 8.1 секунды до 348 миллисекунд, достигая 23-кратного ускорения, сохраняя при этом высокий успех задач как в симуляции, так и на реальном роботе.

en.title: Real-Time Inference with Modality-Aware Step-Distillation
en.desc: Flash-WAM is a new framework designed to improve the efficiency of world-action models (WAMs) by using a modality-aware step-distillation approach. This method adapts consistency functions to different noise levels in video and action streams, allowing for real-time inference. By optimizing the training process for each modality, Flash-WAM significantly reduces the time needed for inference from 8.1 seconds to just 348 milliseconds, achieving a 23-fold speedup. Despite this acceleration, it maintains high task success rates in simulations and recovers strong performance in real-world applications.

zh.title: Flash-WAM：实时推理的新突破
zh.desc: Flash-WAM是一种模态感知的步骤蒸馏框架，旨在实现世界动作模型的实时推理。该框架通过为不同噪声环境适配一致性函数，解决了视频和动作流在联合训练中面临的噪声分布不一致问题。Flash-WAM在LingBot-VA上实现了每个模态的推理压缩到单步，显著提高了推理速度。实验结果表明，Flash-WAM在多个基准测试中保持了高任务成功率，并在真实世界应用中表现出色。

papers.title: Video2LoRA: Parametric Video Internalization for Vision-Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Video2LoRA enables efficient video processing in vision-language models by predicting Low-Rank Adaptation weights from video representations, reducing computational costs while maintaining video-faithful outputs.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Processing video in vision-language models is expensive: each frame occupies hundreds of tokens, and inference cost scales with every frame and every repeated query. We introduce Video2LoRA, a method for parametric video internalization. A perceiver hypernetwork reads the intermediate representations produced layer-by-layer as a frozen VLM encodes a video, and generates a Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) adapter in a single forward pass. Unlike standard LoRA fine-tuning, which requires iterative gradient updates, Video2LoRA predicts these weights directly from the video.
