Trained for SmolVLM2 500M and 2.2B on video summarization and captioning, Video2LoRA enables the same frozen VLM to answer queries from the adapter alone, with zero visual tokens in its context at query time. Video2LoRA is statistically non-inferior and equivalent to direct video-in-context inference across all five captioning benchmarks at both model scales, and across seven of eight video question answering benchmark-scale pairings. Although trained only on 12 frames at 384px, it remains stable up to 1,024 frames and 1024px, where direct video-in-context inference often degenerates. Across this sweep, it reduces answer-time visual-token load by up to 1,500x and query TTFT by 6-80x, while preserving video-faithful outputs. We also find that independently generated adapters for non-overlapping video segments can compose in rank space, suggesting a path toward chunked long-video internalization.

ru.title: Эффективная видеообработка через предсказание адаптивных весов
ru.desc: Video2LoRA предлагает эффективный способ обработки видео в vision-language моделях путём предсказания весов Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) непосредственно из промежуточных представлений видео. Вместо традиционной fine-tuning, которая требует итеративных градиентных обновлений, метод использует гиперсеть типа Perceiver для прямого предсказания параметров адаптера в один проход. Модель достигает статистической эквивалентности стандартному подходу с видео в контексте на эталонах видеосаммаризации и видеовопросов, при этом снижая вычислительные затраты в 6-80 раз за счёт исключения визуальных токенов из контекста запроса. Метод демонстрирует масштабируемость до 1024 кадров при сохранении адекватности выходов видео и возможностью композиции адаптеров для обработки длинных видео.

en.title: Efficient Video Processing with Low-Rank Adaptation
en.desc: Video2LoRA is a novel method that enhances video processing in vision-language models by predicting Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) weights directly from video representations. This approach significantly reduces the computational costs associated with processing each video frame, which typically requires handling a large number of tokens. By utilizing a perceiver hypernetwork, Video2LoRA generates LoRA adapters in a single forward pass, eliminating the need for iterative gradient updates. The method has been shown to maintain performance comparable to traditional video-in-context inference while drastically lowering the visual-token load and improving query response times.

zh.title: 高效视频处理的新方法：Video2LoRA
zh.desc: Video2LoRA是一种高效的视频处理方法，旨在降低视觉语言模型中的计算成本，同时保持视频输出的真实性。该方法通过从视频表示中预测低秩适配器（LoRA）权重，避免了传统LoRA微调所需的迭代梯度更新。Video2LoRA能够在一次前向传递中生成适配器，使得冻结的视觉语言模型可以仅通过适配器回答查询，而无需在上下文中使用视觉标记。经过训练后，Video2LoRA在视频摘要和字幕生成任务中表现出色，显著减少了视觉标记的负担。

papers.title: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract AdaCodec reduces video encoding redundancy by transmitting full visual tokens only when scene prediction fails, otherwise encoding compact inter-frame change descriptions.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Video is temporally redundant: adjacent frames usually share most objects, background, and layout. Yet existing video multimodal large language models (video MLLMs) usually encode each sampled frame as an independent RGB image, causing visual tokens to repeat content already present in earlier frames. This suggests a more direct video interface: send a full reference frame only when the scene cannot be predicted well from prior context, and otherwise transmit a compact description of inter-frame changes. We call this interface a predictive visual code, and instantiate it for video MLLMs as AdaCodec. AdaCodec spends full visual tokens on a reference frame only when its conditional predictive cost is high; otherwise, it encodes inter-frame changes, including motion and prediction residuals, as compact P-tokens. Across all eleven benchmarks, AdaCodec improves over the Qwen3-VL-8B per-frame RGB baseline at a matched visual-token budget. Even at 1/7 the budget, AdaCodec with 32k tokens surpasses the 224k baseline on all long-video benchmarks; on five general-video benchmarks, it raises the average score while substantially cutting time-to-first-token from 9.26s to 1.62s.

ru.title: Адаптивное кодирование видео через предсказание межкадровых изменений
ru.desc: AdaCodec — это метод адаптивного кодирования видео для видео-LLM, который снижает избыточность путём передачи полных визуальных токенов только когда предсказание сцены неудачно. Вместо независимого кодирования каждого кадра как RGB-изображения, система использует полный опорный кадр только при высокой ошибке предсказания, а иначе передаёт компактное описание межкадровых изменений (движение и остатки предсказания) в виде P-токенов. Метод достигает улучшения качества на всех одиннадцати бенчмарках при сопоставимом бюджете визуальных токенов, а при бюджете в 1/7 от исходного превосходит базовую модель на всех долгоформатных видео-бенчмарках. Кроме того, AdaCodec существенно ускоряет время до первого токена с 9,26 секунд до 1,62 секунды.

en.title: Efficient Video Encoding with Predictive Visual Codes
en.desc: AdaCodec is a novel approach to video encoding that minimizes redundancy by only sending full visual tokens when the scene cannot be accurately predicted from previous frames. Instead of treating each frame as an independent RGB image, it leverages the temporal redundancy in video data by encoding compact descriptions of changes between frames. This method, termed predictive visual code, allows for efficient encoding by focusing on inter-frame changes when the predictive cost is low. As a result, AdaCodec significantly outperforms traditional methods in various benchmarks while reducing the time needed to start processing video data.

zh.title: 智能视频编码，提升效率！
zh.desc: AdaCodec通过在场景预测失败时传输完整的视觉标记，减少视频编码的冗余。相邻帧通常共享大部分对象、背景和布局，因此在预测良好的情况下，仅需编码紧凑的帧间变化描述。该方法称为预测视觉编码，旨在提高视频多模态大语言模型的效率。实验结果表明，AdaCodec在多个基准测试中表现优于传统的逐帧RGB编码方法，显著提高了处理速度。

papers.title: Combinatorial Synthesis: Scaling Code RLVR via Atomic Decomposition and Recombination
papers.abstract: Abstract Atomic Decomposition and Recombination (ADR) framework generates novel and challenging verifiable code tasks for scalable reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards in large language models.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has recently emerged as the cornerstone for shaping the remarkable coding abilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). However, the scalability of RLVR is severely constrained by the scarcity of sufficiently challenging verifiable code tasks that target near the model's edge of competence. Prior studies often rely on heuristic seed expansions for data synthesis, which severely limits both novelty and difficulty. Consequently, the training value of such data fails to scale proportionally with the size of its synthesis. To this end, we propose Atomic Decomposition and Recombination (ADR), a novel framework that generates verifiable code tasks via decomposition into atomic elements and controlled recombination, thereby enabling the generation of genuinely novel and challenging verifiable code tasks. Experiments and analysis demonstrate that ADR achieves superior originality, difficulty, diversity, and test quality over existing baselines, and consistently delivers greater improvements in code ability across RLVR in diverse downstream domains, including algorithmic programming, tool usage, and data science. Our work sheds light on a new paradigm for novel code task synthesis and scalable RLVR training.

The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen
The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Разложение и переконфигурация для масштабируемого обучения кодирования
ru.desc: Статья предлагает фреймворк ADR для генерации новых и сложных задач на проверку кода, необходимых для масштабируемого обучения с подкреплением и проверяемыми вознаграждениями в больших языковых моделях. Авторы выявляют проблему нехватки достаточно сложных задач верификации кода, которые соответствуют уровню компетентности LLM, что ограничивает масштабируемость RLVR. Предложенный метод работает путём декомпозиции задач на атомарные элементы и их контролируемой рекомбинации, что позволяет создавать действительно оригинальные и сложные задачи. Эксперименты показывают, что ADR превосходит существующие методы по оригинальности, сложности и качеству, обеспечивая лучшие результаты при обучении моделей в различных областях программирования.

en.title: Revolutionizing Code Task Generation for Scalable RL
en.desc: The paper introduces the Atomic Decomposition and Recombination (ADR) framework, which creates new and challenging verifiable code tasks for scalable reinforcement learning. This framework addresses the limitations of existing methods that rely on heuristic seed expansions, which often produce less novel and difficult tasks. By breaking down tasks into atomic components and recombining them, ADR generates tasks that are more original and diverse, enhancing the training process for Large Language Models (LLMs). The results show that ADR significantly improves the coding capabilities of models trained with Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) across various programming domains.

zh.title: 新框架，挑战代码任务的生成
zh.desc: 原子分解与重组（ADR）框架为可验证奖励的可扩展强化学习生成了新颖且具有挑战性的可验证代码任务。通过将任务分解为原子元素并进行控制性重组，ADR能够生成真正新颖且具有挑战性的代码任务，从而克服了以往方法在数据合成中的局限性。实验结果表明，ADR在原创性、难度、多样性和测试质量上优于现有基准，并在算法编程、工具使用和数据科学等多个下游领域中显著提升了代码能力。我们的研究为新型代码任务合成和可扩展的强化学习训练提供了新的思路。

papers.title: Multimodal Music Recommendation System using LLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract A multimodal framework for session-based music recommendation integrates audio, lyric, and semantic signals with LLM-based sequential reasoning to improve recommendation accuracy.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Music recommendation systems typically treat songs as opaque tokens, relying on collaborative interaction histories which overlooks semantic or acoustic content. Prior work has explored LLM-augmented, multimodal, and text-enhanced approaches to sequential recommendation, and while some methods partially combine semantic, acoustic, or engagement signals, none jointly model all three within a unified LLM-based sequential reasoning framework that grounds recommendations in actual song content. In this work, we propose a multimodal framework for session-based music recommendation that enriches the LastFM-1K dataset with three complementary signals: (1) audio and lyric embeddings extracted using pretrained music and text representation models, (2) LLM-generated semantic metadata using the MGPHot annotation schema, and (3) listening completion ratios. We adopt the E4SRec framework by extending it with multimodal features and different item ID encoder backbones, including SASRec, BERT4Rec, and GRU4Rec. We further extend the LLM backbone option with LLaMa-2-13B, Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, and LLaMa-3-70B in both zero-shot and fine-tuned settings. Our experiments show that integrating content-based features improves over ID-only baselines up to 95% in terms of Recall and 79% in terms of NDCG. Moreover, our experiments show that naive multimodal fusion does not always yield additive improvements, highlighting challenges in cross-modal integration. We release a large-scale multimodal benchmark for music recommendation.

ru.title: Три модальности лучше одной: мультимодальная рекомендация музыки с LLM
ru.desc: В работе предложена мультимодальная система для рекомендации музыки на основе сессий, которая объединяет три типа сигналов: аудио- и лирические эмбеддинги, семантические метаданные, полученные с помощью большой языковой модели, и метрики прослушивания. Авторы расширили фреймворк E4SRec различными энкодерами айтемов (SASRec, BERT4Rec, GRU4Rec) и несколькими LLM-бэкбонами, включая LLaMa и Qwen, протестировав их в нулевой выборке и с файн-тюнингом. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что интеграция признаков контента повышает качество рекомендаций на 95% по метрике Recall и на 79% по NDCG по сравнению с базовыми моделями, основанными только на айтем-айдентификаторах. Исследование выявило, что наивное слияние модальностей не всегда дает дополнительные улучшения, что указывает на сложность кроссмодального интегрирования.

en.title: Harmonizing Music Recommendations with Multimodal Insights
en.desc: This paper presents a new framework for music recommendation that combines audio, lyrics, and semantic information using large language models (LLMs) for better accuracy. Traditional systems often ignore the actual content of songs, treating them as simple tokens based on user interactions. The proposed method enhances the LastFM-1K dataset by incorporating audio and lyric embeddings, LLM-generated metadata, and user engagement metrics. Experiments demonstrate significant improvements in recommendation performance, while also revealing the complexities of effectively integrating different types of data.

zh.title: 多模态音乐推荐：精准与挑战并存
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种多模态框架，用于基于会话的音乐推荐，结合了音频、歌词和语义信号，以提高推荐的准确性。传统的音乐推荐系统通常将歌曲视为不透明的标记，依赖于用户的互动历史，而忽视了歌曲的语义或声学内容。本文通过扩展E4SRec框架，整合了多种特征，包括音频和歌词嵌入、LLM生成的语义元数据以及听歌完成率，从而实现了更精准的推荐。实验结果表明，内容驱动的特征整合显著提升了推荐效果，但简单的多模态融合并不总是能带来预期的改进。

papers.title: Is This Edit Correct? A Multi-Dimensional Benchmark for Reasoning-Aware Image Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract RE-Edit benchmark evaluates image editing systems on five reasoning dimensions to assess logical consistency beyond visual plausibility.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Diffusion-based image editing has achieved strong visual fidelity under natural language instructions, yet most existing systems still operate at the level of surface instruction following, without reasoning about the implicit contextual constraints embedded in real user requests. This often leads to visually plausible but logically inconsistent edits. In this work, we introduce RE-Edit, a benchmark for REasoning-aware image Editing that evaluates image editing systems across five complementary reasoning dimensions: physical, environmental, cultural, causal, and referential. RE-Edit comprises 1,000 carefully curated samples, each designed such that visual plausibility alone is insufficient and correct editing requires satisfying implicit logical constraints. To support fine-grained analysis, we establish dimension-aligned evaluation criteria and conduct a comprehensive study of ten open-source and two commercial image editing models. Our results show that even advanced systems frequently struggle with implicit multi-dimensional reasoning despite producing high-quality visuals. We further present a lightweight reasoning-guided post-edit baseline as an initial exploration, illustrating how inserting explicit reasoning can help mitigate such failures in a model-agnostic manner.

ru.title: Логичное редактирование изображений: от визуального правдоподобия к семантической согласованности
ru.desc: В работе представлен бенчмарк RE-Edit для оценки систем редактирования изображений на основе диффузионных моделей. Бенчмарк проверяет логическую согласованность отредактированных изображений по пяти измерениям рассуждения: физическому, окружающему, культурному, причинному и референциальному. Исследование показало, что даже продвинутые системы часто не справляются с неявными многомерными ограничениями, хотя генерируют визуально правдоподобные результаты. Авторы предложили легкий метод постобработки, управляемый явными рассуждениями, который помогает снизить частоту логических ошибок независимо от архитектуры модели.

en.title: Enhancing Image Editing with Logical Reasoning
en.desc: The RE-Edit benchmark evaluates image editing systems by focusing on their ability to reason logically, not just create visually appealing images. It identifies five key reasoning dimensions: physical, environmental, cultural, causal, and referential, which are essential for understanding user requests. The benchmark consists of 1,000 samples that require more than just visual fidelity; they demand adherence to implicit logical constraints. The study reveals that even advanced image editing models often fail to meet these reasoning requirements, highlighting the need for improved reasoning capabilities in AI systems.

zh.title: 逻辑一致性：图像编辑的新标准
zh.desc: RE-Edit基准测试评估图像编辑系统在五个推理维度上的表现，以评估逻辑一致性超越视觉可信度。尽管基于自然语言指令的图像编辑技术在视觉保真度上取得了显著进展，但许多现有系统仍然仅停留在表面指令的执行上，未能考虑用户请求中隐含的上下文约束。这常常导致视觉上可信但逻辑上不一致的编辑结果。我们提出的RE-Edit基准包含1000个精心策划的样本，旨在通过五个互补的推理维度来评估图像编辑系统的能力。

papers.title: SePO: Self-Evolving Prompt Agent for System Prompt Optimization
papers.abstract: Abstract Self-Evolving Prompt Optimization (SePO) enhances agent performance by jointly optimizing both task and prompt agent system prompts through evolutionary search, demonstrating superior accuracy across diverse benchmarks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct System prompt optimization improves agent behavior without modifying the underlying model, yielding human-readable, model-agnostic instructions. Existing methods build a prompt agent that refines task agents' system prompts, yet leave the prompt agent's own system prompt hand-engineered and fixed. We propose Self-Evolving Prompt Optimization (SePO), which treats the prompt agent's own system prompt as an optimization target alongside task agents' system prompts. SePO adopts a self-referential design. A single prompt agent improves both task agents' system prompts and its own under an open-ended evolutionary search that maintains an archive of candidate prompts as stepping stones. Training proceeds in two stages: pre-training evolves the prompt agent on a multi-task pool, and fine-tuning then applies it to a target task. Across five benchmarks spanning math (AIME'25), abstract reasoning (ARC-AGI-1), graduate-level science (GPQA), code generation (MBPP), and logic puzzles (Sudoku), SePO consistently outperforms Manual-CoT, TextGrad, and MetaSPO, improving the average accuracy by 4.49 points compared to Manual-CoT. The prompt optimization skill from pre-training also generalizes to tasks beyond the pre-training mixture, rather than memorizing per-task prompts.

ru.title: Самоэволюционирующая оптимизация: промпты учатся улучшать самих себя
ru.desc: В статье представлен метод Self-Evolving Prompt Optimization (SePO), который одновременно оптимизирует системные промпты как для агентов задач, так и для самого агента оптимизации промптов с помощью эволюционного поиска. Ключевая инновация заключается в самореферентном подходе, когда единый агент улучшает промпты других агентов и собственный промпт, используя архив кандидатов как ступени развития. Метод предусматривает двухэтапное обучение: предварительное обучение на наборе разнородных задач и последующая тонкая настройка на целевой задаче. SePO демонстрирует улучшение точности на 4.49 процентных пункта по сравнению с базовыми методами на разнообразных бенчмарках, включая математику, логику и генерацию кода, при этом полученные промпты обобщаются на новые задачи.

en.title: Evolving Prompts for Superior AI Performance
en.desc: Self-Evolving Prompt Optimization (SePO) is a method that improves the performance of AI agents by optimizing both the prompts used for tasks and the prompts for the agent itself through an evolutionary search process. This approach allows the prompt agent to adapt and enhance its own instructions while also refining the prompts for task agents, without changing the underlying AI model. SePO operates in two stages: first, it pre-trains the prompt agent on a variety of tasks, and then fine-tunes it for specific tasks, leading to better performance across multiple benchmarks. The results show that SePO significantly increases accuracy compared to existing methods, demonstrating its effectiveness in generating adaptable and human-readable prompts.

zh.title: 自我进化，优化提示，提升性能
zh.desc: 自我进化提示优化（SePO）通过进化搜索共同优化任务和提示代理系统提示，从而提升代理性能，显示出在多种基准测试中具有更高的准确性。该方法在不修改基础模型的情况下，通过生成可读的、与模型无关的指令来改善代理行为。与现有方法不同，SePO将提示代理的系统提示视为优化目标，并在开放式进化搜索中同时改进任务代理的系统提示。通过预训练和微调两个阶段，SePO在多个基准测试中表现优异，平均准确率比手动链（Manual-CoT）提高了4.49个百分点。

papers.title: Quality-Guided Semi-Supervised Learning for Medical Image Segmentation
papers.abstract: Abstract A quality-guided semi-supervised learning framework for medical image segmentation that uses a dedicated quality predictor to improve pseudolabel reliability and segmentation performance.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Training accurate medical image segmentation models requires large amounts of densely annotated data, which is costly and time-consuming to obtain. Semi-supervised learning (SSL) alleviates this by learning from both abundant unlabeled data and limited labeled data. However, most modern SSL methods rely on pseudolabels for unlabeled data, and typically assess their reliability through model confidence or uncertainty, measures that are self-referential and lack explicit grounding in segmentation quality. Instead, we propose a quality-guided SSL framework that trains a dedicated network to estimate segmentation quality from image-mask pairs. The predictor is trained on variable-quality masks generated through synthetic corruptions augmented with imperfect outputs from partially trained segmentation models, capturing realistic error patterns encountered during training. We integrate the quality predictor into SSL through two complementary mechanisms: a quality-aware regularization loss and a quality-based pseudolabel sample reweighting scheme. We show that our method serves as a drop-in enhancement to existing SSL frameworks. Extensive experiments across five datasets and multiple architectures demonstrate consistent improvements over competing SSL methods, advancing the state-of-the-art in semi-supervised medical image segmentation.
affiliations: School of Computing Science, Simon Fraser University, Canada

ru.title: Качество выше доверия: предиктор для надежной полусупервизной сегментации медицинских изображений
ru.desc: В работе предложен фреймворк полусупервизного обучения для сегментации медицинских изображений, который использует специализированный предиктор качества для повышения надежности псевдоразметок. Основная идея заключается в том, что вместо оценки надежности псевдоразметок через уверенность модели, авторы обучают отдельную нейросеть предсказывать качество сегментации на основе пар изображение-маска. Предиктор качества тренируется на синтетически поврежденных масках и реальных ошибках, возникающих во время обучения сегментационной модели. Предложенный метод интегрируется в полусупервизное обучение через две механизма: регуляризационную функцию потерь, взвешенную по качеству, и переобучение псевдоразметок на основе оценок качества.

en.title: Enhancing Medical Image Segmentation with Quality-Guided Learning
en.desc: This paper presents a new approach to improve medical image segmentation using a quality-guided semi-supervised learning (SSL) framework. It introduces a dedicated quality predictor that assesses the reliability of pseudolabels, which are used for training on unlabeled data. By training this predictor on various quality levels of masks, the framework captures realistic error patterns and enhances segmentation performance. The proposed method integrates quality assessment into SSL through regularization and sample reweighting, leading to significant improvements over existing SSL techniques in medical imaging tasks.

zh.title: 质量引导的半监督学习提升医学图像分割
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种质量引导的半监督学习框架，用于医学图像分割。该框架使用专门的质量预测器来提高伪标签的可靠性和分割性能。通过训练一个网络来估计图像-掩膜对的分割质量，结合质量感知的正则化损失和伪标签样本重加权机制，增强了现有的半监督学习方法。实验结果表明，该方法在多个数据集和架构上均优于其他半监督学习方法，推动了医学图像分割的最新进展。
